candidate.design_rationale: A three‐phase inverter using space vector PWM with a current loop has been selected to meet grid-tied requirements at 20 kW. The DC-link is assumed to operate near 700 V which is within the specified range, and each component (controller, power module, gate driver, DC-link capacitor, current sensor, and EMI filter) is chosen to ensure proper voltage and current ratings with sufficient margin. The design intentionally uses conservative modulation and targets low ripple to meet both efficiency and THD constraints.

topology_decision.reason: The three-phase inverter topology best satisfies grid-tied operation at 20 kW with space vector PWM control, offering adequate performance and control margin while meeting voltage, current, and efficiency targets.

execution_log.note: CI-safe DC-AC evaluator path; live waveform replay can be attached as frozen run records.

candidate.design_rationale: The design uses a well-understood three-phase inverter topology that leverages a C2000 grid-tie controller paired with a 1200V-rated power module to bridge a 760-850V DC-link. The selected DC-link capacitor (DCL-1000V-140A) and current sensor (ACS772-100B) sufficiently support low ripple and current measurement needs. An isolated gate driver (ISO5852S) and robust EMI filter (EMI-690V-160A) ensure safe drive and EMC performance for grid-tied operation while meeting efficiency and THD targets.

topology_decision.reason: A three-phase inverter topology was chosen for its proven performance in grid-tied applications, offering straightforward control with space vector PWM and robust current management. The selected components provide adequate voltage and current margins while meeting efficiency and harmonic targets.

execution_log.note: CI-safe DC-AC evaluator path; live waveform replay can be attached as frozen run records.

candidate.design_rationale: This design uses a standard three-phase two-level inverter topology with space vector PWM and a current loop to achieve precise traction auxiliary control. Operating from an assumed DC link voltage of 600 V (within the 520–650 V band), the design employs robust power modules and an isolated gate driver to maintain safety margins. The component selections allow the inverter to deliver around 30 kW at 380 V line-to-line with low THD (<4%) and high efficiency, meeting the conservative performance and protection requirements.

topology_decision.reason: The three-phase inverter topology is well-established for traction applications. It supports space vector PWM with current loop control, meeting efficiency and THD targets while enabling robust protection margins. Its simplicity and extensive industrial validation make it a cost-effective choice for the specified operating range.

execution_log.note: CI-safe DC-AC evaluator path; live waveform replay can be attached as frozen run records.

candidate.design_rationale: This design implements a three‐phase, two-level inverter for grid connection using a high-performance C2000 grid‐tie controller paired with isolated UCC21750 gate drivers and robust power modules. A high-voltage DC link capacitor (rated 1800V) is chosen to ensure low ripple current, while the LEM-HASS200 current sensor and EMI-690V filter provide necessary protection and signal integrity, ensuring conservative margins on modulation index, phase current, and device stress.

topology_decision.reason: A two-level, three-phase topology was selected for its simplicity and proven performance in grid-tie applications, offering adequate device stress headroom and ease of implementation with space-vector PWM.

execution_log.note: CI-safe DC-AC evaluator path; live waveform replay can be attached as frozen run records.

candidate.design_rationale: The design uses a three-phase inverter topology featuring a robust C2000 controller with space vector PWM and current loop, paired with isolated UCC21750 gate drivers to meet safety mandates. The DC-link is maintained by a DCL-700V-90A capacitor to ensure ripple current remains below constraint, and PM-1200V-250A modules provide ample voltage margin and current handling for a 40 kW islanding-capable 480 V microgrid inverter. Current sensing and EMI filtering are conservatively specified to ensure compliance with THD and efficiency targets.

topology_decision.reason: A three-phase inverter best meets the harmonic performance and load distribution requirements for a 40 kW microgrid inverter, while allowing conservative margins with available components.

execution_log.note: CI-safe DC-AC evaluator path; live waveform replay can be attached as frozen run records.

candidate.design_rationale: The design uses a conventional three‐phase two-level inverter employing space-vector modulation with an inner current loop for high-traction applications. A robust C2000-class controller manages the inverter while the PM-1200V-250A modules provide sufficient voltage margin against an 850-1000 V DC link and ensure low conduction losses. The DC-link capacitor (DCL-1100V-260A) is chosen to contain ripple well below the 69.6 A limit, and the isolated UCC21750 gate drivers ensure safe switching with the necessary isolation. Overall, conservative margins are maintained across devices to meet efficiency and THD targets.

topology_decision.reason: Three-phase two-level inverters offer simplicity and effective control in high-power traction applications while fulfilling space vector PWM with current loop requirements.
